message:
fix(pager): prevent concurrent freelist corruption in WAL split-lock commit (beads_rust#138)

Root cause: In the WAL-mode split-lock commit path, freed pages were pushed
into the shared inner.freelist during Phase A (under inner.lock()), then the
lock was dropped for Phase B (WAL I/O). A concurrent transaction could acquire
the lock during this window, observe the uncommitted freed pages, and serialize
a conflicting freelist into its own write_set. When the group commit flusher
wrote both batches, the last writer's page 1 (with stale/inconsistent freelist
trunk pointer and count) overwrote the first writer's, creating orphaned pages
("page N is never used") detectable by PRAGMA integrity_check.

Fix: Freed pages are now drained into a local vec (pending_freed) and passed
to serialize_freelist_to_write_set which builds a predicted freelist from
inner.freelist + pending_freed WITHOUT mutating inner.freelist. The actual
promotion into inner.freelist is deferred to Phase C, after the WAL commit
succeeds. On commit failure, freed pages are restored to self.freed_pages
so rollback can still observe them.

Changes:
- serialize_freelist_to_write_set: new pending_freed_pages parameter; builds
  predicted freelist from a local clone rather than mutating inner.freelist
- commit(): compute freelist_dirty before draining freed_pages; defer
  inner.freelist promotion to Phase C; restore on failure
- commit_and_retain(): same pattern
- Two new regression tests:
  - test_concurrent_freelist_no_orphaned_pages_after_sequential_free_commits
  - test_freed_pages_not_in_freelist_before_commit_success

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
